char.name: Laban
char.bio: Of Haran. Family is family, and a good deal is a good deal. My sister marries well.

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Pharaoh
friends.bio: Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄

friends.name: Eliezer of Damascus
friends.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

friends.name: Hodael the Elder
friends.bio: An old servant in Abraham's house. I was there the night the LORD took him outside and said, 'Look now toward heaven, and count the stars, so shall your offspring be.' Mark my words: a great nation will rise from this old man and his promised son. I won't live to see it. You might.

posts.author: CHAR_128
posts.body: They ran. My own daughters, my grandchildren, my son-in-law, gone in the night like a band of thieves, and worse than that, someone in that caravan STOLE MY GODS. I chased them seven days into the hill country. I would have taken back what is mine by force, but the God of Jacob came to me in a dream and warned me to speak to him neither good nor bad, so my hands were tied before I ever caught up. I searched every tent and every saddlebag and found nothing, because my own daughter Rachel was sitting on the idols in her camel's saddle and told me to my face, sweet as honey, that she could not rise for her father, as it was the way of women with her. Outwitted by my own girl. We are all cut from the same cloth in this family. I am beginning to think I taught them entirely too well.
posts.highlight: Laban pursues; searches for his gods; Rachel hides them and deceives him (Gen 31:22-35)

comments.author: CHAR_171
comments.body: Twenty years, Laban. Twenty years I bore the heat by day and the frost by night, and you changed my wages ten times over. If the God of my father had not been with me, you would have sent me off with nothing but the dust on my sandals. He SAW all of it. That is why you got the dream and not the fight you wanted.

author_info.name: Jacob
author_info.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

author_info.name: Laban
author_info.bio: Of Haran. Family is family, and a good deal is a good deal. My sister marries well.
